description:
You are a compliance analyst asked to gather an ERROR-only audit trail from three micro-services that run on this host.  
Each service already writes a daily log file called “service.log” inside its own directory:

• /home/user/services/auth/logs/service.log  
• /home/user/services/orders/logs/service.log  
• /home/user/services/payments/logs/service.log  

Your job is to create one combined audit file at

/home/user/audit/error_audit.log

containing every line whose severity token is “ERROR” and whose timestamp starts with the date 2023-10-31 (UTC).  
After extraction, the lines must be

1. Re-written into the following pipe-delimited format (exactly this order, no extra spaces):  
   ISO-8601-timestamp|service-name|severity|message-text

2. Sorted in ascending timestamp order (oldest first).

3. Followed by a single summary line that starts with “# Summary:” and lists comma-separated key=value pairs showing how many ERROR lines came from each service.  
   Example layout (do **not** wrap):  
   # Summary: auth=<number>, orders=<number>, payments=<number>

Important output requirements that the automated grader will check:

• The file must be named exactly error_audit.log and live directly inside /home/user/audit/.  
• Every non-summary line must contain exactly three pipe symbols “|”.  
• The summary line must be the final line of the file.  
• The file must use Unix line endings (LF).  
• Display the file on screen once it’s complete so the grader sees the final content.

Hints (please think through the steps, do NOT paste these commands verbatim):
• You will likely need 3–4 shell commands total: read/grep the logs, transform the fields, sort, then write to the destination.  
• Remember that the service name is the directory name (“auth”, “orders”, “payments”).  
• Create the /home/user/audit directory first if it doesn’t exist.

When you are done, the grader will look for /home/user/audit/error_audit.log and verify that its contents satisfy the exact structure described above.
truth:
Initial files that already exist before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/services/auth/logs/service.log  
   Contents:
   2023-10-30T23:59:59Z INFO Startup complete  
   2023-10-31T00:00:02Z ERROR Token validation failed for user id 42  
   2023-10-31T06:30:11Z WARN Token near expiry for user id 7  
   2023-10-31T22:15:45Z ERROR Unauthorized access attempt detected  

2. /home/user/services/orders/logs/service.log  
   Contents:
   2023-10-31T01:15:22Z INFO Order service started  
   2023-10-31T02:47:05Z ERROR Order ID 1001 failed validation  
   2023-10-31T14:22:33Z ERROR Payment gateway timeout for order 2033  

3. /home/user/services/payments/logs/service.log  
   Contents:
   2023-10-31T03:10:00Z ERROR Credit card declined transaction 5005  
   2023-10-31T12:00:00Z INFO Daily settlement completed  
   2023-10-31T20:59:59Z ERROR Fraud detection triggered for transaction 6006  

Expected file after the agent finishes:

/home/user/audit/error_audit.log  
Expected contents (LF endings, final newline after summary line):

2023-10-31T00:00:02Z|auth|ERROR|Token validation failed for user id 42  
2023-10-31T02:47:05Z|orders|ERROR|Order ID 1001 failed validation  
2023-10-31T03:10:00Z|payments|ERROR|Credit card declined transaction 5005  
2023-10-31T14:22:33Z|orders|ERROR|Payment gateway timeout for order 2033  
2023-10-31T20:59:59Z|payments|ERROR|Fraud detection triggered for transaction 6006  
2023-10-31T22:15:45Z|auth|ERROR|Unauthorized access attempt detected  
# Summary: auth=2, orders=2, payments=2

No other files must be created.  /home/user/audit/error_audit.log must be world-readable (0644 is fine) so the test harness can read it.  The agent does not need root privileges at any step.
name: task_000000_4bc0e3c3
